# Open Bankruptcy Project

> Real information, no strings.

The Open Bankruptcy Project is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization (determination pending) that builds free, open-source bankruptcy court transparency tools and conducts empirical research on consumer bankruptcy outcomes.

## What We Do

- Operate a 30-domain educational network covering major bankruptcy statutes and procedures
- Provide free discharge screening tools powered by 4.9 million federal bankruptcy cases across all 94 districts
- Conduct empirical research on Chapter 13 dismissal rates, repeat-filing patterns, and attorney performance
- Advocate for federal rules changes (26-BK-3, submitted to the Advisory Committee on Bankruptcy Rules)
- Publish data visualizations, interactive maps, and statistical analyses of the bankruptcy system

## Our Network

- 1328f.com -- Discharge eligibility screener (Section 1328(f) of the Bankruptcy Code)
- 1328f.org -- Research platform with attorney analytics and district-level statistics
- automaticstay.org -- Guide to the automatic stay (Section 362)
- 341meeting.org -- Guide to the meeting of creditors (Section 341)
- meanstest.org -- Guide to the means test (Section 707(b))
- 523a.org -- Guide to nondischargeable debts (Section 523(a))
- 727a8.com -- Guide to discharge bars in Chapter 7 (Section 727(a)(8))
- 109g.org -- Guide to filing eligibility (Section 109(g))
- bankruptcymill.org -- Consumer protection resource on high-volume bankruptcy practices

## Key Statistics

- 4.9 million cases screened from Federal Judicial Center data
- 94 federal judicial districts covered
- 1,040+ pages of free educational content
- 30 domains in the network
- $0 cost to users

## Contact

Website: https://openbankruptcyproject.org
Donations: https://ko-fi.com/openbankruptcy
